metadata.note: Iteration 3 is the final iteration per JUDGE §11 stop-condition. After this pass, the pipeline proceeds to Step 3 PERTURBATION regardless of verdict.

summary.lycopene_titer_cap_status: 3 activators <= 5 phenotype-cap; 5 inhibitors <= 7 cap (per BUILDER_AGENT.md §10); composite-bundled coverage preferred over hard edge-count reduction.
summary.lycopene_titer_curated_coverage_after: 17/43 = 0.40 (ENVELOPE_STRESS bundles E085-E090 = 6 curated edges; MEMBRANE_STORAGE bundles E082-E084+E134 = 4; retained 7 other direct edges)

edges_added_by_id.N097: ENVELOPE_STRESS -> Lycopene_Titer (-1). Composite edge bundling E085-E090 (WaaC, WaaF, Lpp, NlpI, MlaE, TolA). Evidence: Zhu 2022 (10.3390/ijms23147783), Bongers review (10.3390/biom13121747).
edges_added_by_id.N098: MEMBRANE_STORAGE -> Lycopene_Titer (+1). Composite edge bundling E082 (Plsb), E083 (Plsc), E084 (Almgs), E134 (DgkA). Evidence: Wang 2020 (10.3390/molecules25143136), Hu 2018 (10.1186/s12934-018-0887-x).
edges_added_by_id.N099: NADPH -> Mevalonate (+1) per curated E021. Captures HMGR substrate-flux dependence on NADPH pool.

edges_removed_by_id.N090: ALMGS -> PLSB (+1) -- removed because both endpoint nodes are being collapsed into MEMBRANE_STORAGE composite.
edges_removed_by_id.N091: PLSB -> Lycopene_Titer (+1) -- replaced by MEMBRANE_STORAGE -> Lycopene_Titer (N098) which bundles Plsb evidence with Plsc/Almgs/DgkA.

suggestions_applied.decision: APPLIED Option A (Envelope_Stress composite) + extension (Membrane_Storage composite)
suggestions_applied.rationale: Envelope_Stress composite qualifies under BUILDER §9.3 Composite Node Rules: (1) functional redundancy -- all six members disrupt outer-membrane integrity, (2) same downstream target Lycopene_Titer, (3) same direction of effect (-1). Membrane_Storage composite qualifies similarly: all four members promote membrane phospholipid/glycolipid biogenesis that expands lycopene hydrophobic storage. Both composites use type PROTEIN_COMPLEX to signal the collapse, with multi-edge evidence bundle in the single network edge.
suggestions_applied.coverage_impact: Lycopene_Titer §9.11 coverage 8/43 -> 17/43 = 0.40 (above 0.30 threshold, approaching 0.40 strong-coverage threshold).

suggestions_applied.rationale: E021 NADPH -> Mevalonate (+1) in_model=True, HIGH confidence. Mevalonate had 4 activators; addition takes count to 5 (within 5-7 target). Captures HMGR's NADPH substrate-flux dependence that complements the existing NADPH -> HMGR (enzyme-activity) edge.

suggestions_deferred.rationale: Adding PMK would require restructuring IPP from parallel-activators (currently 7, at cap) to a chain topology, because adding a direct PMK -> IPP edge pushes IPP to 8 activators (hard-cap violation). Chain restructure (Mevalonate -> MVK -> implicit-intermediate -> PMK -> implicit-intermediate -> MVD -> IPP) would require creating intermediate metabolite nodes that are not in curated_edges.json, which would violate the 'every edge has a DOI' rule. BUILDER prefers the current parallel-activator arrangement (which correctly predicts direction of MVA perturbations) over a restructure that would introduce phantom nodes.

Only E069 OxyR -> KatG is curated for the OxyR/KatG pair. There is NO curated KatG -> ROS edge. If OxyR and KatG were added as nodes, KatG would have no outgoing edge and therefore no path to Lycopene_Titer, making KatG a floating node in violation of BUILDER §1.1 Rule 1. OxyR alone similarly dead-ends at KatG. Filed as literature_gap (KatG -> ROS needs a curated evidence sentence from Zhu 2022 or equivalent for Step 1 re-open).

Same cap complication as S005: IspG -> IPP and IspH -> IPP would push IPP to 8-9 activators, exceeding the hard 7-cap. Would require IPP chain restructure with phantom intermediate nodes not curated in literature. DXR addition (from iteration 2) already captures the most literature-prominent interior MEP step; IspG/IspH are MEDIUM confidence and rarely perturbed individually in the lycopene literature.

Judge iter 2 flagged G3P and Pyruvate as cap violations, but re-reading BUILDER §10 shows the rule is 'Max activators per node 5-7' AND 'Max inhibitors per node 5-7' (per-side, not total). G3P has 7 activators + 3 inhibitors (at cap on activators, under on inhibitors). Pyruvate has 7 activators + 3 inhibitors (same). Neither violates the hard rule. ED_Pathway composite merge (EDD+EDA) would be a legitimate biology-preserving cleanup but is not required to satisfy BUILDER constraints. Deferred to REFINEMENT (Step 5) if validator shows signal dilution issues with 7 parallel activators.
Plsc (E083) is now captured inside the MEMBRANE_STORAGE composite bundled evidence. No separate edge needed.

hard_rule_checks.no_floating_nodes: PASS - BFS from Lycopene_Titer reaches all 62 nodes (script check 1/5)
hard_rule_checks.every_edge_has_DOI: PASS - all 94 edges have DOIs (script check 2/5)
hard_rule_checks.node_naming: PASS - all 62 node names match type-specific regex including new composites ENVELOPE_STRESS and MEMBRANE_STORAGE which are PROTEIN_COMPLEX-typed using ALL_CAPS_UNDERSCORE convention (script check 3/5)
hard_rule_checks.is_source_flags: PASS - auto-consistency after is_source reflow in this iteration (script check 4/5)
hard_rule_checks.single_phenotype_node: PASS - Lycopene_Titer is the unique PHENOTYPE node (script check 5/5)
hard_rule_checks.no_fabricated_edges: PASS - all 94 network edges trace to curated_edges.json entries (composites trace to multi-edge bundles)
hard_rule_checks.equation_shape_fixed: PASS - regenerated algebraic + ODE equations use the canonical formulas from BUILDER §13
hard_rule_checks.wt_baseline_1.0: PASS by construction (geometric mean of 1s = 1, bounded inverse of 1 = 1)
